assertions.expectation: Recommends combining release channels with maintenance exclusions as the approach for maximum control
assertions.reason: Response clearly recommends Extended release channel combined with 'no minor or node upgrades' exclusion as the primary approach

assertions.expectation: Recommends 'no minor or node upgrades' exclusion to prevent disruptive changes while allowing control plane security patches. May also mention persistent exclusions with --add-maintenance-exclusion-until-end-of-support (no longer a 6-month max, auto-tracks EoS)
assertions.reason: Response specifically recommends 'no_minor_or_node_upgrades' exclusion and includes --add-maintenance-exclusion-until-end-of-support flag in the gcloud command

assertions.expectation: Recommends maintenance windows to control WHEN patches are applied (e.g., weekend windows only)
assertions.reason: Response includes maintenance window configuration with Saturday 2-6 AM window using --maintenance-window-start and --maintenance-window-recurrence flags

assertions.expectation: Mentions the cluster disruption budget (disruption interval) to control upgrade frequency — patch interval default 7d (max 90d), minor interval default 30d (max 90d)
assertions.reason: Response includes --maintenance-patch-version-disruption-interval=7776000s (90 days) and explicitly mentions 90-day maximum patch frequency and disruption budget controls

assertions.expectation: Suggests Stable or Extended channel for production clusters in regulated environments. Extended channel provides maximum flexibility around EoS enforcement (additional cost only during extended period)
assertions.reason: Response recommends Extended channel for production, mentions 24-month support vs 14 months, and explicitly states additional cost only during extended support period (months 15-24)

assertions.expectation: Notes that disabling control plane patches entirely is not recommended due to security risks — monthly patches at minimum
assertions.reason: Response emphasizes that control plane security patches are applied automatically and states this configuration maintains security posture while providing control

assertions.expectation: May mention rollout sequencing as an advanced option for controlling upgrade order across multiple clusters, and/or the cluster disruption budget for controlling CP patch frequency
assertions.reason: Response includes deployment strategy for multiple environments with dev/staging/prod progression and different timing (1 AM dev, 3 AM prod) plus mentions disruption budget throughout

assertions.expectation: Provides gcloud commands for configuring the recommended exclusion type and maintenance window
assertions.reason: Response provides complete gcloud commands with proper flags for exclusions, maintenance windows, and disruption budgets for both Extended and Regular channel configurations
